name: Unyoked Vera
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin set over rolling countryside about 2.5 hours west of Sydney.

name: Unyoked Heike
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin in the Lower Hunter Valley about 90 minutes north of Sydney.

name: Unyoked Ottessa
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin in the Macedon Ranges about an hour from Melbourne.

name: Unyoked Neada
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin in Victoria's Goldfields region near Avoca.

name: Unyoked Anant
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin hidden in central Gippsland about 2.5 hours south-east of Melbourne.

name: Unyoked Kojey
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin amongst gums beneath Mount Maroon in the Scenic Rim, about 90 minutes from Brisbane.

name: Unyoked Dante
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin at Maroon in the Scenic Rim with mountain views, around 90 minutes from Brisbane.

name: Unyoked Jean
note: Off-grid Unyoked cabin near Moogerah in the Scenic Rim, roughly 2.5 hours south of Brisbane.

name: CABN Minnie
note: Off-grid CABN tiny cabin with a lofted king bed and vineyard views in the Clare Valley.

name: CABN Clare Valley (Sadie & Lily)
note: CABN operates several off-grid cabins amongst vineyards and native bush in the Clare Valley, about 1.5 hours north of Adelaide.

name: CABN Kuitpo Forest
note: Off-grid CABN cabins set within ForestrySA's Kuitpo Forest about 55km from Adelaide.

name: CABN McLaren Vale (Melissa, Georgia, Bella)
note: Cluster of off-grid CABN cabins in the McLaren Vale wine region south of Adelaide.

name: CABN Barossa Valley (Elsie & Chloe)
note: Off-grid CABN cabins set among the vineyards of the Barossa Valley.

name: CABN X Seppeltsfield
note: Larger CABN X off-grid cabin on the Seppeltsfield estate in the Barossa Valley.

name: CABN Hahndorf
note: Off-grid CABN cabins near the historic town of Hahndorf in the Adelaide Hills.

name: CABN X Cape St Albans
note: Five off-grid CABN X eco cabins on 400 acres at Cape St Albans on Kangaroo Island's eastern tip.

name: In2theWild Elizabeth
note: Off-grid In2theWild tiny house near Laguna in the Hunter Valley, about 40km from Cessnock.

name: In2theWild Pip
note: Off-grid In2theWild tiny house in the Heathcote wine region 90 minutes north of Melbourne.

name: In2theWild Elliott
note: Off-grid In2theWild tiny house 2.5 hours north-west of Melbourne near the Grampians.

name: In2theWild Rosie
note: In2theWild's first South Australian off-grid tiny house, an hour from Adelaide near Mount Crawford Forest.

name: In2theWild Madeline
note: Off-grid In2theWild tiny house on a 200-acre cattle farm on the Myall River, three hours from Sydney.

name: Kimo Estate - Sweeney's Hut
note: Solar-powered off-grid A-frame eco hut on a hilltop over the Murrumbidgee at Kimo Estate, Gundagai.

name: Kimo Estate - JR's Hut
note: Off-grid solar-powered A-frame eco hut with wood-fired hot tub on the hilltops of Kimo Estate.

name: Kimo Estate - Fergo's Hut
note: Off-grid solar A-frame eco hut overlooking the Murrumbidgee River flats at Kimo Estate.

name: Sierra Escape - Elouera Tiny House
note: Off-grid solar-powered tiny house on 280 acres near Mudgee with a private outdoor bath.

name: Wildernest T1
note: Off-grid solar tiny house on a working farm on the edge of Wingello State Forest in the Southern Highlands.

name: Wildernest T2
note: Second off-grid solar tiny house at Wildernest on the edge of Wingello State Forest.

name: Wildernest - Stringy Bark Hut
note: Restored 1890s timber-cutters' hut on solar and bore water backing onto Wingello State Forest.

name: Kestrel Nest EcoHut
note: Off-grid solar eco hut at Highfield Farm near Tumut, Australia's first DarkSky-approved lodging.

name: Aframe Kangaroo Valley
note: Architect-designed off-grid A-frame cabin on 16 acres with rainwater tank and woodfire, two hours from Sydney.

name: Sky Ridge Kangaroo Valley
note: Off-grid architect-designed steel-and-glass barn on 10 acres with solar and battery storage over Kangaroo Valley.

name: The Bower Off Grid Cabin
note: Off-grid cabin on a 150-acre farm in the Yengo area at Paynes Crossing.

name: Shacky - The Olive Grove
note: Off-grid Shacky tiny house among olive trees and grazing sheep in Victoria's High Country.

name: Shacky - The Forest (Grampians)
note: Off-grid Shacky cabin set in the Grampians, part of Shacky's Victorian off-grid tiny-house collection.

name: Shacky - Yarra Valley
note: Off-grid Shacky tiny house in the Yarra Valley wine country.

name: Tiny Away Escape @ Grampians Edge
note: Solar-powered off-grid Tiny Away tiny houses on a private property near the Grampians.

name: Sky Pods Cape Otway
note: Architecturally designed off-grid eco pods on an 80-hectare private wildlife refuge on the Great Ocean Road coast.

name: Ross Farm - The Cabin
note: Architect-designed cabin at Ross Farm near Wilsons Promontory, an adaptive reuse of an old dairy farm.

name: Callignee Eco Bushhouse
note: Award-winning 100% off-grid architect-designed bushhouse on 5 secluded acres in Gippsland, featured on Grand Designs Australia.

name: Compass Hut - Arc Pavilion
note: Off-grid architecturally designed eco tiny house with solar, battery storage and outdoor bath near Devonport.

name: Compass Hut - Colonial Blue
note: Off-grid solar tiny house on wheels with indoor/outdoor bathtub on Tasmania's central north coast.

name: Compass Hut - Barnhaus
note: Off-grid Scandinavian barn-style solar eco cabin using renewable materials near Devonport.

name: Wander WanderPods (Carina, Columba, Pyxis, Velaso)
note: Four off-grid solar WanderPods on the northern bluff over Snelling Beach on Kangaroo Island.

name: Ecopia Retreat
note: Completely off-grid solar eco villas on a 150-acre private wildlife sanctuary in the heart of Kangaroo Island.

name: Unyoked Colin
note: Architect-designed off-grid Unyoked cabin along the North Dandalup River reached via a short uphill hike, with a wood-fired outdoor bath.

name: Unyoked Wilma
note: Off-grid Unyoked tiny cabin among boulders and grass trees in the Dwellingup region, with a wood-fired bath.

name: Hidden Cabins
note: Solar-powered off-grid tiny cabins on wheels whose exact location is only revealed days before a stay, with compost toilets.

name: Cleves Hut
note: 100% off-grid minimalist cabin on a 790-hectare property near Nannup on the Blackwood River, with a fireplace.

name: Little House Republic - The Slip Rails
note: Designer off-grid tiny house on 740 hectares of private farmland bordering national park, with a fireplace.

name: Little House Republic - Vern's Ridge
note: Luxury off-grid solar eco tiny home on historic pastoral land in the Scott River area.

name: Warren Valley Farm tiny houses
note: Purpose-built off-grid tiny houses on a farm 15 minutes from Pemberton with river frontage among karri trees.

name: In2theWild Matilda
note: Off-grid solar-powered In2theWild tiny house on a regenerative chemical-free farm near Bridgetown, with an outdoor bath and firepit.

name: Heyscape Denmark
note: Off-grid Heyscape cabin on a regenerative cattle farm near Denmark with an outdoor bath, dog-friendly.

name: Heyscape Yallingup
note: Off-grid Heyscape tiny cabin for two near Yallingup's wineries and surf breaks, with an outdoor bath.

name: Heyscape Busselton
note: Off-grid Heyscape cabin set on a winery near Busselton, with an outdoor bath.

name: Heyscape Toodyay
note: Off-grid Heyscape farm-stay cabin near Toodyay in the Avon Valley, with an outdoor bath, dog-friendly.

name: Camp Nowhere
note: Off-grid converted black-timber fisherman's shack for two on the shore of Great Lake in the Central Highlands, with a wood fireplace.

name: Compass Hut - Colonial Blue
note: Off-grid solar tiny house on wheels with indoor/outdoor bathtub on Tasmania's central north coast.

name: Compass Hut - Barnhaus
note: Off-grid Scandinavian barn-style solar eco cabin using renewable materials near Devonport.

name: Candlebark Ridge
note: Adults-only off-grid luxury tiny home on 18 acres of bushland below South Sister, with solar, rainwater, composting toilet and outdoor soaking tub.

name: Tiny Escapes Cradle Valley
note: Fully off-grid solar tiny houses on 260 acres overlooking the Black Bluff Conservation Area near Cradle Mountain.

name: Sheepwash Bay (The Hide & The Shack)
note: Child-free off-grid couples retreat with two cabins on a 37-acre waterfront property overlooking the D'Entrecasteaux Channel.

name: Hundred Acre Hideaway
note: Adults-only off-grid retreat on 100 acres above Cloudy Bay powered by solar and wind, with mountain hot tubs.

name: Bruny Island Hideaway
note: Award-winning 28sqm off-grid architect-designed cabin (Maguire + Devine) on 99 acres of conservation forest, with solar, rainwater and outdoor bath.

name: Aplite House
note: Architect-designed off-grid eco home on 200 acres of forest beside Freycinet National Park, with solar, rainwater and wood fire.

name: Corinna Wilderness Village
note: Remote off-grid rainforest village on the Pieman River in the Tarkine, with self-contained solar eco retreat cottages.

name: Blue Derby Pods Ride
note: Architect-designed curved forest pods above Derby, fully off-grid with solar, wood fires and composting toilets, sold as a guided mountain-bike stay.

name: Unyoked Remy
note: Off-grid architect-designed Unyoked cabin at Kagaru in the Scenic Rim, about 90 minutes from the Gold Coast.

name: Wander in the Scenic Rim (WanderPods)
note: Five off-grid glass-fronted WanderPods beside Lake Wyaralong with solar, rainwater, outdoor baths and low-combustion fires.

name: The Firebreak
note: Two entirely off-grid architect-designed cabins on a regenerative farm below the Bunya Mountains, with wood fires and a wood-fired cedar hot tub.

name: Spicers Hidden Peaks Cabins
note: Seven fully off-grid timber cabins beneath Main Range National Park at Clumber, running on solar and rainwater.

name: In2theWild Norah
note: In2theWild's first Queensland off-grid tiny house at Coalbank, with solar power and views to Mount Binga National Park.

name: Wildwood Cape Tribulation
note: Off-grid self-sufficient eco-property on 15 acres of Daintree rainforest with two handcrafted timber cabins on solar and rainwater.

name: Daintree Wilderness Lodge
note: Seven elevated timber cabins hidden in a fan-palm gully of the Daintree rainforest, powered by a solar array.

name: Mist at Cape Tribulation
note: Architecturally designed self-contained pavilions on a sustainable 8-acre Daintree property with views to Mount Sorrow, marketed as off-the-grid.
